NUM_WORKERS = 0"},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"","old":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=total_steps)","new":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max(1, total_steps))"},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        stem_channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        stage2_channels = BASE_CHANNELS * CHANNEL_MULT
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM

        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, stem_channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(stem_channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)

        self.stage1 = nn.Sequential(
            *[
                ResidualBlock(stem_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM)
                for _ in range(max(1, DEPTH))
            ]
        )

        downsample_layers: list[nn.Module] = [
            nn.Conv2d(stem_channels, stage2_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias)
        ]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            downsample_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(stage2_channels))
        downsample_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.downsample = nn.Sequential(*downsample_layers)

        self.stage2 = nn.Sequential(
            *[
                ResidualBlock(stage2_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM)
                for _ in range(max(1, DEPTH))
            ]
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(stage2_channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.stage1(x)
        x = self.downsample(x)
        x = self.stage2(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)"}]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"topk","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The one-cycle run regressed, so the safest next move is to reduce the base step size rather than change the model again; this keeps the schedule but makes early updates less aggressive.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4"}]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","primary_mode":"micro","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"],"rationale":"Restore the more stable cosine setup that worked best so far, and raise the batch size so each fixed step processes more CIFAR-10 examples. This is the least disruptive way to try to beat the current best after the one-cycle regression.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3"},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128"},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"","old":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=max(1, total_steps),
        pct_start=0.2,
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=100.0,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        cycle_momentum=False,
    )","new":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max(1, total_steps))"}]}
